title: IELTS Academic Mock Test 1
description: Complete IELTS Academic test with all 4 skills

passages.title: Electroreception

vocabList.definition: To become aware of through the senses
vocabList.example: Fish perceive electrical signals in water.
The fish responds to electrical stimuli.

vocabList.definition: A material that allows electricity to pass through
vocabList.example: Water is an efficient conductor of electricity.

vocabList.definition: An animal that hunts other animals for food
vocabList.example: Sharks are top predators in the ocean.
The jamming avoidance response is a fascinating mechanism.
As the shark reaches proximity to its prey, it attacks.

content: A Open your eyes in sea water and it is difficult to see much more than a murky, bleary green colour. Sounds, too, are garbled and difficult to comprehend. Without specialised equipment humans would be lost in these deep sea habitats, so how do fish make it seem so easy? Much of this is due to a biological phenomenon known as electroreception – the ability to perceive and act upon electrical stimuli as part of the overall senses. This ability is only found in aquatic or amphibian species because water is an efficient conductor of electricity.

B Electroreception comes in two variants. While all animals (including humans) generate electric signals, because they are emitted by the nervous system, some animals have the ability – known as passive electroreception – to receive and decode electric signals generated by other animals in order to sense their location.

C Other creatures can go further still. However, animals with active electroreception possess bodily organs that generate special electric signals on cue. These can be used for mating signals and territorial displays as well as locating objects in the water.

subTasks.instruction: Which paragraph contains the following information?

questions.text: how electroreception can be used to help fish reproduce
a possible use for electroreception that will benefit humans

subTasks.instruction: Complete the summary. NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS.

questions.text: Electroreception is found mainly in ___ creatures.

passages.title: The Role of Universities in Modern Society

vocabList.example: A degree is a prerequisite for many jobs.
Universities must cater to diverse student needs.
Academic autonomy allows universities to set their own curricula.
Tuition fees are a major revenue source.

content: A Universities have traditionally been seen as institutions dedicated primarily to the transmission of knowledge and the preparation of students for professional careers. However, in recent decades, their role has expanded considerably to include research, community service, and economic development.

B The modern university is expected to fulfill multiple functions. It must provide high-quality education while simultaneously producing cutting-edge research. This dual mission sometimes creates tensions, as faculty members must balance teaching responsibilities with research demands.

C Another significant change has been the dramatic increase in university enrollment rates worldwide. What was once an elite privilege available to a small percentage of the population is now becoming a mass phenomenon. This expansion has raised questions about quality maintenance and the value of degrees in the job market.

D Funding models have also evolved. While traditional public universities relied heavily on government subsidies, many now derive substantial revenue from private sources, including tuition fees, corporate partnerships, and research grants. This shift has implications for institutional autonomy and academic freedom.

subTasks.instruction: Choose the correct heading for each paragraph.

questions.text: Paragraph A
Paragraph B
Paragraph C
Paragraph D

subTasks.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the views of the writer?

questions.text: Universities only need to focus on teaching nowadays.
More people attend university than ever before.

passages.title: Climate Change Impacts on Global Agriculture

vocabList.example: Farmers must adapt to changing weather.
Sustainable farming practices protect the soil.
Drought can reduce crop yields significantly.

content: A Climate change poses significant challenges to agricultural systems worldwide. Rising temperatures, changing precipitation patterns, and increasing frequency of extreme weather events are affecting crop yields and food security across the globe. These changes require fundamental adjustments in farming practices and agricultural policies.

B The impacts vary significantly by region. In some areas, warming temperatures may extend growing seasons and allow new crops to be cultivated. However, in many tropical and subtropical regions, higher temperatures combined with reduced rainfall are likely to decrease productivity and threaten food supplies for millions of people.

C Adaptation strategies include developing heat-tolerant crop varieties, improving irrigation efficiency, and diversifying agricultural systems. Additionally, farmers can adopt conservation agriculture practices that help soils retain moisture and nutrients. These approaches can help maintain productivity even under challenging conditions.

D Mitigation efforts in agriculture focus on reducing greenhouse gas emissions while also enhancing carbon sequestration in soils and biomass. Sustainable practices such as agroforestry, cover cropping, and improved fertilizer management can contribute to both climate mitigation and adaptation goals.

subTasks.instruction: Complete the sentences below. NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS.

questions.text: Climate change affects ___ patterns and crop yields.
Tropical regions may experience ___ in productivity due to heat.
___ agriculture helps soils retain moisture.

subTasks.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C, or D.

questions.text: What is mentioned as a potential benefit of warming temperatures in some areas?
Which practice helps both mitigation and adaptation?

sections.title: Task 1: Data Description
sections.instruction: Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

sections.title: Task 2: Essay
sections.instruction: Write about the following topic. Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your knowledge or experience.
sections.prompt: Some people believe that unpaid community service should be a compulsory part of high school programs. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

sections.title: Section 1: Conversation
sections.transcript:
Man: Hello, I'm looking for information about the university library...
Woman: Of course, I'd be happy to help. What would you like to know?

questions.text: What time does the library open?
The library is closed on ____

parts.title: Part 1: Introduction and Interview
parts.instruction: Answer the examiner's questions about familiar topics.

questions.text: What kind of place is it?
What's the most interesting part of your town/village?
Would you say it's a good place to live? (Why?)
Tell me about the kind of accommodation you live in.
How long have you lived there?

parts.title: Part 2: Long Turn
parts.instruction: You will have 1 minute to prepare, then speak for 1-2 minutes on the topic.

topicCard.title: Describe a skill you learned when you were a child.
cueCards: And explain why this skill was useful to you

parts.title: Part 3: Discussion
parts.instruction: Discuss more abstract related topics with the examiner.

questions.text: What skills do you think are important for children to learn?
Do you think children learn better from teachers or from their parents?
